recalls.Description: Doosan Bobcat North America has announced a recall for Bobcat and Steiner AirFX Finish Mower Deck attachments due to a potential crush hazard. The mower decks are entirely black and feature the label "AirFX" along with either "72" or "61" to denote the model size. This recall includes specific model numbers which can be found on the right-side frame rail at the rear of the attachment. The affected models are 7526575 (72" AirFX), 7526579 (61" AirFX), 7449223 (61" AirFX Finish Mower), and 7447341 (72" AirFX Finish Mower).
recalls.Title: Doosan Bobcat North America Issues Recall for Bobcat & Steiner AirFX Finish Mower Deck Attachments Due to Crush Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers should reach out to Doosan Bobcat North America at 800-743-4340, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional details can be found online at www.bobcat.com/na/en/parts-service/service/recalls or by visiting www.bobcat.com and selecting "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Bobcat AirFX Finish Mower Deck Attachment
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 552 (In addition, about 27 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Bobcat & Steiner AirFX Finish Mower Deck attachment

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of five incidents where the locking pins were unable to be installed to secure the deck in the upright or tilted position. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Doosan Bobcat North America, of West Fargo, North Dakota

Retailers.Name: Available through Bobcat and Steiner dealers across the country from April 2023 to September 2024, priced between $5,200 and $5,500.

Hazards.Name: Inability to secure mower decks in upright or tilted positions during maintenance, leading to crush hazards.

Remedies.Name: Inspect locking pins for proper insertion capability; contact Doosan Bobcat for free repairs or obtain safety straps for secure maintenance.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Doosan Bobcat recalls AirFX Finish Mower Decks due to crush hazards. Inspect and contact for remedies.

recalls.Description: TTI Outdoor Power Equipment is recalling specific models of RYOBI Brushless 21" Cordless Walk-Behind Self-Propelled Multi-Blade Push Mowers (40V HP) due to a potential fire hazard. The affected mowers feature a black cutting deck and gray top housing, along with a black bag marked with the "RYOBI" logo. This recall includes models RY401014BTLUS, RY401014US, RY401140US, RY401015BTLUS, RY401015US, RY401150US, RY401140US-Y, RY401150US-Y, RY401020, and RY401200, with serial numbers ranging from KC21032D010001 to KC21327N999999, located inside the green mower housing.
recalls.Title: RYOBI Battery-Powered Mowers Recalled by TTI Outdoor Power Equipment Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact TTI at 800-597-9624, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or visit https://ryobi.ryobitools.com/support/safety or https://www.ryobitools.com/ and select "Important Recall Information" for further details.

Products.Name: RYOBI Brushless 21" Cordless Walk-Behind Mowers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 217,500 (In addition, about 28,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled RYOBI Mowers--Models RY401015BTLUS, RY401015US, RY401150US, RY401150US-Y, RY401020, and RY401200
Recalled RYOBI Mowers-Models RY401014BTLUS, RY401014US, RY401140US, RY401140US-Y (Canada)

Injuries.Name: TTI has received 97 reports of overheating while the mower was in use, including five reports of fires. Two minor burn injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: TTI Outdoor Power Equipment Inc., of Anderson, South Carolina

Retailers.Name: Available at Home Depot and Direct Tools Factory Outlet stores nationwide, as well as online at www.homedepot.com and www.directtoolsoutlet.com from February 2021 to January 2025, priced between $500 and $700.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating push-on connector inside the powerhead

Remedies.Name: Stop using the mower immediately and contact TTI for instructions to disable the mower and obtain a free replacement

recalls.metaDescription: TTI recalls RYOBI battery-powered mowers due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact TTI for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Kawasaki Motors USA has announced a recall for specific John Deere ZTrak Zero Turn Mowers equipped with Kawasaki engines. The affected models include Z720E, Z730M, Z740R, and Z760R, produced between certain serial number ranges. These mowers may experience a failure in the voltage regulator, which can lead to engine overheating and present fire and burn hazards.
recalls.Title: Kawasaki Motors USA Issues Recall for John Deere ZTrak Zero Turn Mowers Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Deere & Company at 800-537-8233 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday to Friday and 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET Saturday, or visit www.deere.com and navigate to 'Recalls' under 'Parts & Service'. Alternatively, reach out to Kawasaki Motors Corp. USA at 866-836-4463 during the same hours or visit www.kawasakienginesusa.com/product-recall.html for further details.

Products.Name: John Deere ZTrak Zero Turn Mower Z720E

Images.Caption: Recalled John Deere ZTrak Zero Turn Mower

Injuries.Name: Kawasaki has received five reports of fires and 26 reports of melting and/or smoking. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Kawasaki Motors Manufacturing Corp. U.S.A. (engine manufacturer), of Maryville, Missouri

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized John Deere dealers across the U.S. from October 2018 to September 2024, priced between $8,000 and $11,500.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to potential engine overheating

Remedies.Name: Free repair through an authorized John Deere dealer; consumers should cease use immediately.

recalls.metaDescription: Kawasaki recalls John Deere ZTrak Mowers due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free repair.

recalls.Description: Stanley Black & Decker is recalling specific models of DeWALT Battery walk-behind mowers due to a potential laceration hazard. The affected models include the 21" Push mowers (DCMWP234U2, DCMWP600X2) and the 21" Self-Propelled mowers (DCMWSP256U2, DCMWSP650Y2). These mowers are characterized by their black, gray, and yellow colors, with the DeWALT logo prominently displayed in yellow. The model number can be found on the rear door, while the date code is located on the left side, above the left rear wheel, indicating the manufacturing date. Only mowers with date codes from 2023 32-58 to 2024 13-58 are impacted; those made after March 2024 are safe.
recalls.Title: DeWALT Battery Walk-Behind Mowers Recalled by Stanley Black & Decker Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact DeWALT at 800-990-6421, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET. More information can also be found online at https://www.dewalt.com/push-mowers-recall or by visiting https://www.dewalt.com and navigating to the 'Safety Notices and Recalls' section.

Products.Name: DeWALT Battery Push Mower
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 46,200 (In addition, about 9,600 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled 2024 DeWALT Battery Push walk behind mowers, model no. DCMWP234U2
Recalled 2024 DeWALT Battery Push walk behind mowers, model no. DCMWP600X2
Recalled 2024 DeWALT Battery Self-Propelled walk behind mowers, model no. DCMWSP256U2
SRecalled 2024 DeWALT Battery Self-Propelled walk behind mowers, model no. DCMWSP650Y2
The model number is printed on the rear door of the mower
The date code is printed on the left side of the mower, above the rear wheel

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of mowers failing to shut off when the bail handle is released and one report of a mower starting without a key inserted. The incident in which a mower started without a key inserted resulted in a finger laceration for which no medical attention was required.

Manufacturers.Name: Stanley Black and Decker Inc., of New Britain, Connecticut

Retailers.Name: Available at The Home Depot, Tractor Supply, Ace Hardware, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at www.homedepot.com, www.tractorsupply.com, and www.acehardware.com from January to July 2024, priced between $530 and $750.

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to potential mower malfunction when wet

Remedies.Name: Free repair available at authorized service centers

recalls.metaDescription: Stanley Black & Decker recalls DeWALT Battery walk-behind mowers due to a laceration hazard. Check your model and contact for repairs.

recalls.Description:
Kawasaki Motors USA has announced a recall of engines used in various lawn and garden equipment brands, including Bad Boy Mowers, BigDog, Bobcat, Cub Cadet, Hustler, John Deere, and Kubota. The affected engine models are from the 2021-2023 production years and include FJ400D, FS600V, FS651V, FX691V, FX730V, FX801V, FX850V, and FXT00V. Consumers can find the engine model and serial numbers on the engine fan housing. A comprehensive list of affected serial numbers is available on Kawasaki's website.

The engines pose a risk of overheating, which could lead to fire and burn hazards. Owners of the affected lawn and garden equipment are urged to stop using their products immediately and reach out to the respective manufacturers or authorized dealers for a free repair. Kawasaki and the associated mower brands are actively contacting known purchasers to ensure safety compliance.
recalls.Title: Kawasaki Motors USA Issues Recall for Engines in Bad Boy Mowers, BigDog, Bobcat, Cub Cadet, Hustler, John Deere, and Kubota Equipment Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Kawasaki Motors Corp. USA at 866-836-4463 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.kawasakienginesusa.com/product-recall for more details. Bad Boy Mowers owners can check their mower's status at https://badboycountry.com/kawasaki-recall. John Deere customers should call 800-537-8233 or visit www.deere.com for recall information. For BigDog, Cub Cadet, and Hustler, contact 888-848-6038 or visit their respective websites for more details. Kubota owners can call 888-458-2682 or go to https://www.kubotausa.com/service-support/safety/safety-notices for assistance.

Products.Name: Kawasaki Engines in Bad Boy Mowers

Injuries.Name: Kawasaki has received eight reports of fires with the recalled engines. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Kawasaki Motors Manufacturing Corp. U.S.A., of Maryville, Missouri

Retailers.Name: Kawasaki and authorized dealers nationwide from April 2021 to December 2023, priced between $4,000 and $15,500

Hazards.Name: Overheating leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the equipment and contact the manufacturer for a free repair

recalls.metaDescription: Kawasaki recalls engines in various lawn and garden equipment due to fire hazards. Immediate action required for affected owners.

recalls.Description: American Honda Motor is expanding its recall to include additional lawnmowers and replacement engines due to a potential injury hazard. This recall affects the Honda HRN216 and HRX217K6 lawnmowers, as well as GCV170/200 G5B engines used in pressure washers and as replacement engines for these lawnmowers. The affected lawnmowers are identifiable by their red and black design and the "HONDA" label on the clipping collection bag. Model and serial numbers can be found on a label on the mower deck's upper rear, while the engines have their serial numbers engraved on a flat surface.
recalls.Title: Honda Expands Recall of Lawnmowers and Engine Units Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach American Honda Motor at 888-888-3139, available from 9 a.m. to 7:30 p.m. ET Monday through Friday. Alternatively, inquiries can be sent via email through https://crmshonda.my.salesforce-sites.com/service/pew2c, or visit https://powerequipment.honda.com/ and select "Recalls and Updates" at the bottom of the page. More details can also be found at https://cdn.powerequipment.honda.com/pe/pdf/Recalls/kp4.pdf.

Products.Name: Honda HRN216 Lawnmower
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 200 additional lawnmower replacement engines (About 391,800 Lawnmowers and Pressure Washers Engines were previously recalled in September 2023)

Images.Caption: Recalled Honda Lawnmower Replacement Engine
Recalled Honda HRN216 Lawnmower
Recalled Honda HRX217K6 Lawnmower

Injuries.Name: Honda has received 2,966 reports of incidents related to camshaft failures since the last recall, in addition to the 2,197 reported incidents in the September 2023 recall, including 38 reports of minor injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: American Honda Motor Co. Inc., of Torrance, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury from sudden retraction of starter rope due to improperly manufactured camshafts.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled lawnmowers and pressure washers and contact a Honda Power Equipment dealer for a complimentary inspection and repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Honda recalls lawnmowers and engines due to injury risks. Stop using affected products and contact Honda for a free inspection.
